interfaces._note: Clock and reset ports are documented in clock_reset_summary, not duplicated here.

control.name: valid_i
control.description: Input handshake — data valid. When valid_i & ready_o are both high in the same cycle, the operands are latched and the FSM exits IDLE.

control.name: ready_o
control.description: Input handshake — module ready. High only in IDLE state. When busy, ready_o = 0 (no pipelining).

control.name: signed_op_i
control.description: Signed operation select: 1 = signed (two's complement) division, 0 = unsigned division. Latched with operands on input handshake.

control.name: valid_o
control.description: Output handshake — result valid. High in OUTPUT state, waits for ready_i to complete the transfer.

control.name: ready_i
control.description: Output handshake — downstream ready. When valid_o & ready_i are both high, transfer completes and FSM returns to IDLE.

control.name: div_by_zero_o
control.description: Divide-by-zero exception flag. Asserted when divisor_i == 0. In this case quotient_o = 64'hFFFF_FFFF_FFFF_FFFF, remainder_o = dividend.

control.name: overflow_o
control.description: Overflow exception flag. Asserted only for INT64_MIN / -1 in signed mode. quotient_o = INT64_MIN, remainder_o = 0.

control.name: busy_o
control.description: Module busy flag. High in all non-IDLE states. When busy_o = 1, ready_o = 0 (no pipelining).

data.name: dividend_i
data.description: Dividend A (numerator). 64-bit, signed or unsigned per signed_op_i. Latched on input handshake.

data.name: divisor_i
data.description: Divisor D (denominator). 64-bit, signed or unsigned per signed_op_i. Latched on input handshake.

data.name: quotient_o
data.description: Quotient Q. 64-bit result. For signed operations, sign = dividend_sign XOR divisor_sign.

data.name: remainder_o
data.description: Remainder R. 64-bit result. Sign follows dividend sign.

parameters.name: WIDTH
parameters.description: Operand width in bits. All primary datapath widths (dividend, divisor, quotient, remainder) derive from this.

parameters.name: PR_WIDTH
parameters.description: Partial remainder register width in bits. |PR_int| < 2^65, so 68-bit signed (bits[67:65] are sign extension) is sufficient.

parameters.name: Q_WIDTH
parameters.description: OTF Q/QM accumulator width. Equals 2 × MAX_ITER = 64. |D|=1 short-circuit guarantees K ≤ 62, so N_max = 32.

parameters.name: MAX_ITER
parameters.description: Maximum iteration count. Set by N = (K+3)>>1, K_max = 62 (|D|=1 short-circuit prevents K=63).

parameters.name: QDS_PR_BITS
parameters.description: PR estimate width for QDS lookup (signed). pr_est = pr_q[66:60], 7-bit signed, range [-64, +63].

parameters.name: QDS_DIV_BITS
parameters.description: Divisor estimate width for QDS lookup. d_hat = D_norm[62:60], 3-bit, range [0, 7].

parameters.name: LZC_BITS
parameters.description: Leading-zero-counter output width. Range [0, 64], so 7 bits (ceil(log2(65))).

clocks.name: clk
clocks.description: Primary system clock. Target frequency 2.5 GHz (400 ps period). All sequential logic clocked on rising edge.

resets.name: rst_n
resets.description: Asynchronous reset, active low. Resets FSM to IDLE state, clears all datapath registers (PR, Q/QM, iter_cnt).

error_handling.saturation_behavior: Not applicable — integer division saturates by exception rather than clamping results.
error_handling.fault_recovery: No automatic recovery. Module returns to IDLE after output handshake completes. Software must re-issue the operation with valid operands.

sw_visible_behavior.description: Software initiates division by presenting dividend_i, divisor_i, signed_op_i with valid_i=1 when ready_o=1.
Results (quotient_o, remainder_o) become available when valid_o=1. Software asserts ready_i=1 to consume and unlock the module.
Software can poll busy_o to determine if the module is available for a new operation. When busy_o=0 (IDLE), ready_o=1.
div_by_zero_o exception: quotient saturated to all-1s, remainder = dividend. Software must check this flag before using results.
overflow_o exception: INT64_MIN / -1 case. Quotient = INT64_MIN, remainder = 0. Only fires in signed mode.

internal_only_behavior.description: 6-state machine: IDLE → PREPARE → ITERATE → CORRECT → CORRECT2 → OUTPUT. Not exposed to software.
Carry-save PR update: pr_4x_sum=pr_sum<<2, pr_4x_carry=pr_carry<<2 → QDS estimation → mult select → CSA 3:2 compression. Runs N iterations.
pr_sum_q (68-bit), pr_carry_q (68-bit, LSB=0) in CSA mode. Combined to pr_full_q in CORRECT stage via 68-bit CPA.
Dual 64-bit Q and QM registers updated every iteration per q_digit: QM = Q - 1 always holds.
lzc_A = clz64(|A|), lzc_D = clz64(|D|). K = lzc_D - lzc_A. A_norm = |A|<<lzc_A, D_norm = |D|<<lzc_D.
N = (K+3)>>1, shift = 2N-2-K ∈ {0,1}. Controls iteration count and quotient realignment.
Five priority-ordered shortcuts: D=0, INT64_MIN/-1, A=0, |A|<|D|, |D|=1. Bypass ITERATE/CORRECT entirely.
PR-based remainder calculation: pr_full → sign correction → Q_red selection → shift extraction → barrel-shift right by lzc_D+2 or lzc_D+1.
Output multiplexing for sign: signed_op_i selects signed/unsigned path. Quotient sign = dividend_sign XOR divisor_sign. Remainder sign = dividend_sign.

ambiguities.item: Output signal behavior during IDLE/not-valid: Are quotient_o and remainder_o held at last valid result, or zeroed, or undefined between operations? The spec does not state reset value of output registers.
ambiguities.impact: Low — downstream logic should only sample when valid_o=1, but affects formal verification and initial observability.

ambiguities.item: ready_i backpressure: When valid_o=1 and ready_i=0, does the module hold outputs stable indefinitely? Likely yes (standard handshake), but not explicitly stated for all corner cases.
ambiguities.impact: Low — standard practice, but could affect deadlock analysis if the downstream consumer stalls.

ambiguities.item: SRT-8 upgrade path: The project roadmap mentions SRT-8 extension but the current architecture (PR_WIDTH=68, Q_WIDTH=64, MAX_ITER=32, QDS table) is specific to radix-4. The migration impact on datapath widths, QDS table size, and iteration formula has not been assessed.
ambiguities.impact: Medium — future architecture decisions (e.g., unified vs separate QDS tables) depend on whether SRT-8 reuses or replaces the SRT-4 datapath.

ambiguities.item: Timing closure at 2.5 GHz: The CORRECT stage 68-bit CPA had WNS = -6836 ps at 2 GHz (500 ps) with ASAP7 RVT xp33 cells. At 2.5 GHz (400 ps), the iteration loop (<500 ps at 2 GHz) still has margin, but the CPA path and overall timing closure strategy need re-evaluation.
ambiguities.impact: Medium — affects whether CORRECT CPA needs further pipelining or cell-type upgrades (SLVT, larger drive) to achieve 2.5 GHz.
